summary: Test changes for Task 02 are correct and meaningful. The TestNew count 24->26 is right (verified: all 24 pre-existing views are available on PG16, plus the 2 new PG16-gated views). Both guard tests (TestNew_StatIOView/TestNew_StatIOTimeView) pin the correct stable map fields matching tech-spec Data Models (stat_io: Ncols 16, DiffIntvl [4,14], OrderKey 4, UniqueKey 0; stat_io_time: Ncols 10, DiffIntvl [4,8]) and pass the litmus test under mutation. The {version:160000, total:26} row is correct (verified 26 available on PG16, 24 on PG14). The Configure dispatch for both new views is exercised by TestViews_Configure via the v.Query != "" invariant. One non-blocking gap: the PG18 branch of SelectStatIOQuery is not asserted at the view layer (no PG16+/PG18 row checks views["stat_io"].QueryTmpl), unlike how the test pins QueryTmpl/Ncols for replication and databases_general.

findings.issue: The TestViews_Configure version matrix tops out at PG14, so the Configure() dispatch for stat_io/stat_io_time is only weakly exercised: on PG14 both SelectStatIOQuery (returns PgStatIOPG16) and SelectStatIOTimeQuery resolve to the same constants already present in the static map, and the only assertion touching them is the generic `assert.NotEqual(t, "", v.Query)` invariant. The version-aware branch of SelectStatIOQuery — `if version >= PostgresV18 { return PgStatIOPG18, ... }` — is never selected by any test row at the view layer. There is no row asserting `views["stat_io"].QueryTmpl == query.PgStatIOPG18` on PG18, unlike the explicit QueryTmpl/Ncols pinning done for `replication` (e.g. PgStatReplicationExtended) and `databases_general` (PgStatDatabaseGeneralPG11). Mutation check confirmed: breaking the `case "stat_io"` dispatch to an empty template IS caught by TestViews_Configure (empty Query fails the invariant), but a wrong-but-non-empty template on the PG18 branch would pass undetected. Note this PG18 selector logic is unit-owned by Task 01 (internal/query); if Task 01 tests assert the PG18 vs PG16 split, this view-layer gap is acceptable.
findings.recommendation: Add a PG18 row to the TestViews_Configure matrix (e.g. `{version: 180000, recovery: "f", trackCommit: "on", querylen: 256}`) and a `case 180000:` asserting `assert.Equal(t, query.PgStatIOPG18, views["stat_io"].QueryTmpl)`, `assert.Equal(t, 16, views["stat_io"].Ncols)`, and `assert.Equal(t, [2]int{4,14}, views["stat_io"].DiffIntvl)`. Optionally add a PG16 row asserting `views["stat_io"].QueryTmpl == query.PgStatIOPG16` to lock the lower branch. This makes the Configure dispatch assert the selected version-aware constant rather than only its non-emptiness.

findings.issue: TestView_VersionOK includes {version:160000} but has no {version:150000} row. The boundary between gated (PG15) and available (PG16) for the new views is the key behavior introduced by this task (MinRequiredVersion=PostgresV16). PG15 (150000 < 160000) would still gate the 2 new views, so total should remain 24 — this exact boundary case is asserted only on one side (160000 passes, the immediately-below 150000 is untested). The litmus is partly satisfied since 140000->24 and 160000->26 already bracket the threshold, so this is a completeness nicety, not a real gap.
findings.recommendation: Optionally add `{version: 150000, total: 24}` to make the gating boundary explicit (PG15 must NOT count the two new PG16 views). Low value given 140000/160000 already bracket the threshold; include only if cheap.

litmusTest.notes: Mutation-verified: TestNew_StatIOView catches wrong Ncols (16->99) and static-map DiffIntvl ([4,14]->[0,0]); TestNew_StatIOTimeView catches removal of the track_io_timing hint from Msg; TestNew catches the 24/26 count; TestView_VersionOK count verified against runtime enumeration (26 on PG16, 24 on PG14). The `assert.Contains(t, Msg, "track_io_timing")` is behaviorally load-bearing (tech-spec Decision 9 mandates the hint), not a shallow string-presence anti-pattern.
